title: Immorta Bio Reports >70% Lifespan Extension in Aging Mice with SenoVax + StemCellRevivify Combo
description: Published in the Journal of Translational Medicine, Immorta Bio's two-arm regenerative therapy—pairing a senolytic vaccine (SenoVax) with a stem cell rejuvenation platform (StemCellRevivify)—produced over 70% lifespan extension in middle-aged animals, one of the largest combined senolytic + cell-therapy effects reported to date. The combination approach validates pairing clearance of senescent cells with positive regenerative stimulation as a translational template.

title: Rhodiola rosea Identified as a Natural Senolytic with Whole-Body Anti-Aging Effects
description: Juntendo University researchers publishing in iScience show salidroside-rich Rhodiola rosea extract acts as a true senolytic in middle-aged mice—improving exercise capacity, reducing hair loss/graying, and reversing skin decline. As a widely available adaptogen, the finding is rapidly translatable to consumer geroprotector protocols and reinforces the natural-products senolytic pipeline alongside fisetin and quercetin.

title: Naked Mole Rat HA Gene Transferred to Mice Extends Lifespan 4.4%, Cuts Cancer 34%
description: A landmark cross-species genetic transfer study shows expressing the naked mole rat high-molecular-weight hyaluronic acid synthesis gene in standard lab mice yields a 4.4% increase in median lifespan, 12.2% increase in maximum lifespan, and a 34% reduction in tumor burden. Researchers frame it as proof-of-concept that exceptionally long-lived rodent biology can be cleanly ported to mammals via single-gene intervention.

title: Reprogramming Wave Crests: Retro, NewLimit, Life Bio, Altos All Cross Major Milestones
description: Fight Aging's weekly commentary highlights an unprecedented confluence of partial-reprogramming capital and clinical progress: NewLimit's $435M Series C, Life Biosciences' first human dose with ER-100, Retro Biosciences' continued expansion, and Altos Labs' platform build-out. The piece argues 2026 is the inflection year where partial reprogramming moves from speculative biology to clinical reality.

title: Medscape Major Feature: 'Inside the Quest to Make Old Cells Young Again' Goes Mainstream
description: Medscape's full editorial feature on cellular rejuvenation surveys the field across Life Bio, Altos, NewLimit, Calico, and academic labs—now widely indexed and being read by practicing clinicians. The piece marks longevity biotech's transition from niche to standard physician-education content and is expected to drive new clinician interest in biological-age testing and rejuvenation referrals.

title: Immorta Bio: >70% Increased Longevity from Patented SenoVax + StemCellRevivify Combo
description: Peer-reviewed paper in Journal of Translational Medicine details the combination senolytic vaccine plus stem-cell rejuvenation platform driving >70% lifespan extension in aged mice.

title: Rhodiola rosea Acts as Natural Senolytic in Middle-Aged Mice
description: Juntendo team (iScience) shows the adaptogen's salidroside compound clears senescent cells and reverses physical aging markers including hair loss, graying, and exercise capacity.

title: Cycloastragenol from Astragalus Shows Senolytic Anti-Aging Effects
description: Chinese team publishing in IJMS finds cycloastragenol selectively clears senescent cells and extends healthspan markers in aged rodents.

title: ABT-263 Senolytic Rejuvenates Aging Skin and Enhances Wound Healing
description: Topical and systemic senolytic ABT-263 (navitoclax) reverses skin atrophy and accelerates wound closure in aged mice, supporting senolytics for dermatological aging.

title: Naked Mole Rat HA Gene Boosts Mouse Lifespan and Cuts Cancer 34%
description: Mice expressing the naked mole rat HMW-HA synthesis gene gained 4.4% median and 12.2% max lifespan with a 34% drop in tumor burden—first cross-species rodent longevity gene transfer of its kind.

title: Hebrew University CRISPR Targets vgll3 Antagonistic Pleiotropy Gene
description: Israeli team uses CRISPR editing of the vgll3 locus to disentangle early-life fertility from late-life decline, validating antagonistic pleiotropy as a druggable longevity axis.

title: ETH Zurich's GRK2 Inhibitor Compound 10 Improves Alzheimer's Models
description: Newly disclosed GRK2 small-molecule inhibitor reduces neuroinflammation and improves cognitive markers in Alzheimer's mouse models—adding a fresh target to the neurodegeneration toolkit.

title: Fight Aging: A Great Deal More Funding for Partial Reprogramming
description: Weekly editorial argues partial-reprogramming capital (Retro, NewLimit, Altos, Life Bio) has crossed a tipping point and the field is now driven by clinical execution rather than fundraising risk.

title: Human Continuum Closes $5.13M Seed for Exosome Longevity Platform
description: Jupiter, FL–based Human Continuum secures over $5M to advance integrated exosome-based therapeutic longevity products targeting biological age reversal.

title: Lifespan Vision Ventures Portfolio Co Rejuvenate Bio Closes $6M + Merck Animal Health Pact
description: Rejuvenate Bio—the dog aging gene therapy spinout from George Church's lab—lands $6M financing and an R&D collaboration with Merck Animal Health to advance veterinary longevity products.

title: BioBucks Tracks 2026 Longevity Biotech VC Funding Surge
description: Updated 2026 venture funding tracker shows longevity-aligned biotechs raised record capital this week led by NewLimit, Retro Biosciences, and Human Continuum.

title: Medscape Feature: Inside the Quest to Make Old Cells Young Again
description: Full editorial feature reaching mainstream clinicians surveys cellular rejuvenation programs at Life Bio, Altos, NewLimit, and academic labs—signaling longevity's transition to physician mainstream.

title: TechTonic Times Deep Dive: Life Bio's ER-100 First-in-Human Trial
description: Long-form analysis of the ER-100 partial reprogramming Phase 1 trial (NCT07290244), including dosing rationale and biomarker readout timeline.

title: GreekReporter: 'Making Aging Cells Young Again' — Life Bio Human Trial
description: International press coverage of Life Biosciences ER-100 first-in-human dose extends mainstream awareness of partial reprogramming as clinical reality.

title: The Star: Surging Scientific Interest in Slowing Biological Aging
description: Feature surveys biological-age testing adoption among physicians and consumers, citing the growing evidence base behind clinical longevity practice.

title: Verve-102 Base-Editing Cholesterol Trial: 18-Month Durable LDL Reduction
description: Updated readout of Verve Therapeutics' base-editing single-dose cholesterol therapy shows continued ~50% LDL reduction at 18 months—proof of concept for permanent genetic edits in adults.

title: PEARL Rapamycin Trial Published: 48-Week Healthspan Gains in Healthy Adults
description: AgelessRx's PEARL trial of low-dose intermittent rapamycin completes peer review, reporting healthspan benefits including lean mass and pain reduction across 48 weeks of dosing.

title: Stanfield-Kaeberlein Rapamycin Trial Reaches Enrollment Milestone
description: The longitudinal rapamycin healthspan trial led by Matt Kaeberlein and Mikhail Stanfield reaches a key recruitment milestone for biomarker readouts.

title: NewLimit Closes $435M Series C to Scale Reprogramming Platform
description: Brian Armstrong–backed NewLimit lands one of the largest single rounds in longevity biotech history to advance its partial reprogramming pipeline.

title: 60 Minutes: Dog Aging Project Highlights Rapamycin in Companion Animals
description: National prime-time feature on the Dog Aging Project introduces a mainstream US audience to canine rapamycin trials and biological-age testing.

title: Einstein BIO-VITAL Trial Launches Metformin + Lifestyle in Older Adults
description: Nir Barzilai's group activates BIO-VITAL, a registrational-style trial combining metformin with structured lifestyle interventions to measure clinical aging endpoints.

title: Forever Healthy Foundation Releases Evipedia Evidence Map
description: Updated open evidence library curating clinical-grade longevity interventions with stoplight risk/benefit ratings—new release covers rapamycin and senolytics protocols.

title: Manulife Commits $350M to Healthspan-Linked Insurance Initiatives
description: Major life insurer expands biological-age-linked underwriting and member wellness programs, signaling deeper insurance industry interest in longevity science.

title: Monash NOX4 Paper in Science Advances Links Oxidative Stress to Brain Aging
description: Monash team identifies NOX4 enzyme as a tractable target for reducing brain oxidative damage during aging, with druggable inhibitor candidates already in preclinical evaluation.

title: FDA Approves Bemotrizinol Sunscreen Filter — Implications for Photoaging
description: First new US UVA-protective filter in decades clears regulatory review, expected to improve real-world photoaging protection alongside conventional sunscreens.

title: UCSD Semaglutide Trial Shows Geroprotective Signals Beyond Weight Loss
description: Investigator-initiated trial of low-dose semaglutide in non-diabetic adults reports favorable inflammatory and biological-age markers independent of weight reduction.

title: Sinclair + Tally Health Publish Combined Biological-Age Evidence Map
description: David Sinclair's group and Tally Health release a joint white paper aggregating clinical-grade interventions ranked by biological-age impact across DunedinPACE and similar clocks.

title: Stanford Wyss-Coray Lab Reports Organ-Chip Aging Model
description: Tony Wyss-Coray's lab unveils a multi-organ chip system reproducing tissue-specific aging trajectories, enabling personalized intervention testing.
